item.title: Tencent-Backer IDG Capital Sets Sights on $2 Billion Growth Fund
item.description: IDG Capital, an investment firm that has backed Tencent Holdings Ltd. and Coinbase Global Inc., is seeking to raise about $2 billion for a growth fund, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Singapore Tops Indonesia as Biggest Southeast Asian Stock Market
item.description: Indonesia continues to notch one grim milestone after another. The latest: losing its status as Southeast Asia’s largest stock market to Singapore.

item.title: EU Expedites Start of US Trade Deal in Bow to Trump Demand
item.description: The European Union finalized the text of its long-delayed US trade deal after months of negotiations, clearing a major hurdle to ratifying the pact before President Donald Trump’s threatened deadline to impose higher tariffs.

item.title: Kokusai Electric Shares Drop as KKR Offloads Its Entire Stake
item.description: Kokusai Electric Corp. shares dropped after saying its biggest holder KKR & Co. is offloading its entire 10.6% stake in the Japanese supplier of chipmaking gear.

item.title: Australia’s Beach Energy Sees M&A Potential on Domestic Gas Plan
item.description: Australia’s Beach Energy Ltd. believes it’s a more attractive acquisition target due to a government plan to force gas producers to keep a portion of their sales at home.

item.title: Jefferies Hires Volschenk to Lead Metals and Mining in EMEA
item.description: Jefferies Financial Group Inc. has hired Gideon Volschenk from Standard Chartered Plc to head its metals and mining banking team in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Bank Indonesia Rate Hike in Play as Rupiah Plumbs New Lows
item.description: Indonesia may see its first interest-rate hike in two years as the rupiah’s slide to record lows this week intensifies pressure for the central bank to mount a stronger defense of the currency.

item.title: Meta Begins 8,000 Global Job Cuts in Asian Hub of Singapore
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is alerting thousands of employees that they’re being laid off, part of a restructuring aimed at improving efficiency and reducing costs while investing heavily in artificial intelligence.

item.title: Threat Looms for Japan’s Stocks as Benchmark Bond Yield Nears 3%
item.description: Japanese stocks are under pressure as long-term interest rates approach 3%, levels which are stoking fears about a hit to corporate earnings via increased borrowing costs.

item.title: Toll Brothers Up As Luxury-Home Orders Hit Highest in Two Years
item.description: Toll Brothers shares rose as much as 5% in postmarket trading on Tuesday after the luxury homebuilder reported second-quarter profit that beat analysts’ estimates and raised its full-year guidance.

item.title: Crucial Japan Bond Sale to Test Market as Global Selloff Deepens
item.description: Investors are on tenterhooks going into a sale of 20-year Japanese government bonds on Wednesday after concerns over inflation and government spending drove super-long yields to multi-year highs.

item.title: Fed’s Paulson Says Rate Cuts Require Progress on Inflation
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia President Anna Paulson said she favored holding interest rates steady and conditioned lower borrowing costs on making sustained progress on inflation.

item.title: Investment Bank Lincoln International’s IPO Raises $421 Million
item.description: Mid-market investment bank Lincoln International Inc. and its backers raised nearly $421 million in an US initial public offering priced at the top of the marketed range.

item.title: Goldman Sachs to Lead SpaceX’s Mega-IPO Bank Lineup
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. has the leading role on the cover of SpaceX’s initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, giving the firm a prominent position in potentially the biggest listing of all time.

item.title: Gold Holds Losses as Iran Impasse Keeps Rate Hike Bets High
item.description: Gold held a decline as a lack of progress in reopening the Strait of Hormuz continued to fuel concerns over inflation and increase bets that global central banks may hike interest rates.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Track US Drop on Inflation Fears: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were set to follow Wall Street lower after mounting inflation concerns pushed the yield on the longest-dated US Treasury bond to its highest level since 2007.

item.title: Oil Holds Decline as Traders Weigh Trump’s Latest Iran Threats
item.description: Oil held a modest decline, as traders shrugged off President Donald Trump’s newest threat to resume strikes on Iran, a pledge the US leader has made repeatedly before backing off since a truce was agreed to in early April.

item.title: SpaceX Is Said to Plan to Buy Startup Cursor 30 Days After IPO
item.description: SpaceX expects to proceed with its acquisition of artificial intelligence coding startup Cursor 30 days after Elon Musk’s company begins trading publicly, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: SoftBank Insiders Worry About Masayoshi Son’s Bet on OpenAI
item.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, why Masayoshi Son’s $60 billion investment in OpenAI is raising concerns inside SoftBank.

item.title: SoftBank Founder’s Starstruck Bet on OpenAI Raises Concern
item.description: With more than $60 billion committed, some SoftBank insiders are growing uneasy over Son’s devotion to Altman.

item.title: Japan Spent Billions and Yen Is Still Falling
item.description: For the full experience visit: Japan Spent Billions and Yen Is Still Falling

item.title: Itochu Takes Over AirLife’s Japan Sales in Healthcare Expansion
item.description: A subsidiary of Japanese trading house Itochu Corp. will take over SunMed Group Holdings’ Japan sales operations, as the US medical equipment maker struggles to remain profitable in the country due to the weak yen and inflationary pressure.

item.title: Analog Devices to Acquire Empower in $1.5 Billion Transaction
item.description: Analog Devices Inc. agreed to acquire closely held Empower Semiconductor for $1.5 billion in cash as the need skyrockets for efficient chips to power data centers required for artificial intelligence.

item.title: Massive Options Bet Rattles Oil Market On Edge Over Iran War
item.description: A huge options bet Tuesday on Brent crude prices plunging rattled oil traders already on high alert for unusual flows, as Iran war headlines continue to whipsaw prices and regulators probe suspicious trading.

item.title: Big Treasury Block Sales Drive ‘Capitulation’ Selloff in Bonds
item.description: A series of large sales in US Treasury futures exacerbated a selloff in the $31 trillion government debt market, as concern about resurgent inflation pushed investors to price in higher interest rates.

item.title: Hasbro Cancels Dungeons & Dragons Game From ‘Star Wars’ Veteran
item.description: Independent studio Giant Skull had announced a deal with Hasbro’s Wizards of the Coast last year.

item.title: Aspen Group Eyes New Investors to Address Debt as Earnings Slump
item.description: The Aspen Group, the owner of one of the largest dental care chains in the US, is exploring tapping outside investors to tackle the maturities of roughly $3 billion of loans due next year amid weaker earnings, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Rising Heat Strains US Power Grid in Need of Off-Season Repairs
item.description: A blast of late-spring heat is adding strain to the largest US power grid as operators race to repair equipment ahead of rising demand and hotter-than-normal weather expected this summer.

item.title: Wall Street Watchdogs Pause Some Cyber Exams After Mythos Shock
item.description: US regulators are pausing some cyber-related examinations of the largest banks, giving the lenders more breathing room as the firms dig into the risks exposed by Anthropic PBC’s new Mythos AI model.

item.title: Now Even AI Is Seeing Growing AI Concentration Risk
item.description: It’s not just stocks.
